reasoning_content: We need to determine if 439 is prime. Check divisibility by small primes: 2: no, odd. 3: sum digits 4+3+9=16, not divisible by 3. 5: doesn't end in 0 or 5. 7: 7*62=434, remainder 5, so not divisible. 11: 11*39=429, remainder 10; 11*40=440, so no. 13: 13*33=429, remainder 10; 13*34=442, so no. 17: 17*25=425, remainder 14; 17*26=442. 19: 19*23=437, remainder 2. 23: 23*19=437, remainder 2; 23*20=460. 29: 29*15=435, remainder 4; 29*16=464. 31: 31*14=434, remainder 5; 31*15=465. sqrt(439) is about 20.95, so we only need to test primes up to 19. We tested 2,3,5,7,11,13,17,19. None divide. So 439 is prime. Answer yes.
